name: Qatari Balaleet (Sweet Saffron Rice with Egg)
steps: Boil the noodles until tender, then toss with the sugar and cardamom.
Scramble the eggs separately and serve alongside.

ingredients_full.note: pinch, steeped in 1 tbsp hot water

steps_detailed: Boil the vermicelli 3-4 minutes until just tender, then drain well.
Melt the butter, stir in the sugar, cardamom, saffron and its water, and rosewater, then toss in the vermicelli until coated and lightly caramelized, 3-4 minutes.
Push the noodles to one side of the pan; scramble the eggs plain on the other side, keeping the two separate.
Serve side by side, scattered with pistachios.
description: Balaleet, sweet saffron vermicelli served alongside plain egg, is a beloved Gulf breakfast - the sweetness of the noodles against the plain egg is the whole point, eaten together in the same bite rather than mixed.

name: Qatari Cheese-Stuffed Chebab
steps: Whisk the flour, egg, and milk into a smooth batter.
Cook small rounds on a hot, lightly oiled griddle; when the top sets, scatter cheese over half and fold.
Cook until the cheese softens and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour, egg, milk, saffron, and cardamom into a smooth, pourable batter and rest 10 minutes.
Cook small, thick rounds on a hot, lightly oiled griddle, 2 minutes; scatter the cheese over half while the top is still wet, fold, and press gently.
Cook 1-2 more minutes until the cheese softens through - a savory turn on chebab, common in Qatari homes alongside the more familiar honey-drizzled version.
description: Chebab, a slightly sweet, pancake-like flatbread, a common Gulf breakfast staple.

name: Qatari Khameer Bread with Date Drizzle
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Pat into a round and cook on a hot, lightly oiled griddle until puffed and golden on both sides.
Blend the dates with the melted ghee and a splash of water; drizzle over the warm bread.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft, slightly tacky dough. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Pat into a round about half an inch thick and cook on a hot, lightly oiled griddle, 3-4 minutes per side, until deeply golden.

Blend the dates with the melted ghee, cardamom, and a splash of warm water until smooth, and drizzle generously over the warm bread - khameer paired with dates rather than cheese is just as common at Qatari breakfast tables.
description: Khameer, a near-daily bakery staple across Qatar, drizzled here with date syrup.

name: Qatari Chicken Machboos
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, turmeric, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken in the oil, 8-10 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic in the same pot, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, cardamom, and pepper, and cook 3-4 minutes.
Return the chicken with 1.75 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and saffron, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Lift out the chicken, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until tender.
description: Fluff the rice and top with the chicken - the pierced dried lime is what separates real machboos from a plain spiced pilaf.

name: Qatari Madrouba (Creamy Rice and Chicken Porridge)
steps: Simmer everything together in 2 cups water for 30-35 minutes until thick.
Shred the chicken through before serving.

ingredients_full.note: cooked well past tender and mashed

steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion, cardamom, turmeric, and pepper in 2.5 cups water, 25-28 minutes, until the chicken is very tender.
Lift out the chicken, shred it, and set aside.
Add the rice to the same broth and simmer 20-25 minutes, well past normal doneness, mashing occasionally, until it breaks down into a thick, smooth, almost porridge-like mass.
Stir the shredded chicken and butter back through.
Madrouba, meaning roughly 'beaten' or 'mashed', is deliberately cooked past the point rice normally stops - the soft, unified texture is the entire point, not a sign of overcooking.
description: Madrouba, meaning roughly 'beaten' or 'mashed', is deliberately cooked past the point rice normally stops - the soft, unified texture is the entire point.

name: Qatari Fish Machboos
steps: Simmer the rice with the tomatoes and turmeric in 1.5 cups water, cover, and cook 20-22 minutes.
Pan-fry the fish separately and serve over the rice.
steps_detailed: Pan-fry the fish in a little of the oil until just cooked through, 3-4 minutes per side; set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic in the remaining oil, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, and cardamom, and cook 3-4 minutes.
Add 1.5 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and saffron, and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the rice, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes until tender.
Fluff the rice, top with the fish, and scatter with cilantro.
description: The fish version of machboos, built the same way as the chicken version - rice cooked in a spiced, dried-lime-perfumed stock.

name: Qatari Thareed
steps: Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes until tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Tear the flatbread into a bowl and ladle the chicken and broth over it, letting it soak.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the potato, tomatoes, onion, and turmeric in a splash of water for 28-32 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Tear the flatbread into a wide serving bowl and ladle the chicken, vegetables, and broth generously over it, letting the bread soak up the liquid.
Thareed's association with the Prophet Muhammad gives it a place of particular respect at Qatari family tables, especially during Ramadan.
description: Thareed, bread soaked in a rich meat-and-vegetable broth, remains a Friday-lunch staple in many Gulf households, eaten communally.

name: Qatari Ghuzi (Spiced Lamb over Rice)
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion and cardamom.
Add the rice and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a few large chunks

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Brown the lamb with the onion in the oil, 9-11 minutes.
Add the cardamom, cinnamon, saffron, and 2 cups water, cover, and simmer 35-40 minutes until very tender.
Lift out the lamb, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.
Toast the almonds 2-3 minutes until golden.
Mound the rice, top with the lamb, scatter with almonds and raisins.
description: Ghuzi, whole spiced lamb served over rice and topped with nuts and raisins, is a classic Gulf celebration centerpiece - traditionally a whole roasted lamb, this keeps the same rice, spicing, and nut-and-raisin topping at a practical scale.

name: Qatari Lamb Machboos
steps: Brown the lamb with the onion.
Add the rice, tomatoes, turmeric, and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-22 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: formed into a few large chunks

steps_detailed: Brown the lamb in the oil, 9-11 minutes, then set aside.
Soften the onion and garlic, 6-8 minutes, until deeply golden.
Add the tomatoes, cinnamon, cardamom, and nutmeg, and cook 3-4 minutes.
Return the lamb with 2 cups water, the pierced dried limes, and saffron, cover, and simmer 40-45 minutes until very tender.
Lift out the lamb, stir the rice into the liquid, cover, and cook over low heat 18-20 minutes.
Fluff the rice and top with the lamb.
description: Lamb machboos, richer and slower-cooked than the chicken version, is a centerpiece of Gulf celebration meals.

name: Qatari Chicken Saloona
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1 cup water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until the potato is tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, garlic, cumin, and turmeric over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the potato, tomatoes, and 1.25 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until the potato is fully tender.
Saloona, ladled loose over rice, is the everyday dinner staple in Qatari homes when the week calls for something simpler than machboos.
description: Saloona, a simple everyday tomato-and-vegetable chicken stew, is common weeknight cooking across the Gulf.

name: Qatari Baked Fish with Rice Stuffing
steps: Mix the cooked rice with the softened onion.
Top the fish with the rice mixture and bake at 400F for 18-20 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: or a whole small fish if available

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Soften the onion in the oil, 5-6 minutes, then mix with the cooked basmati, raisins, almonds, cardamom, and cinnamon.
Season the fish with the lime juice, pack the rice mixture over and around it in a small baking dish.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 20-25 minutes until the fish flakes easily and the rice topping is lightly golden.
Sayadieh-adjacent baked fish over spiced, fruit-and-nut rice is a coastal Qatari classic, built on the same basmati-and-warm-spice base as machboos but finished in the oven rather than the pot.
description: A baked fish and rice dish, common in Qatar's coastal towns.

name: Qatari Samboosa
steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, cut into strips, fill, and fold into triangles.
Fry until golden and crisp.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, cumin, and cilantro.
Make a simple, firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin.
Cut into strips, fill, and fold into tight triangles, sealing the edges well.
Fry until deeply golden and crisp, 4-5 minutes - trays of samboosa appear at nearly every Qatari gathering, especially through Ramadan.
description: Samboosa, fried meat-filled pastries, are ubiquitous at Gulf gatherings, especially through Ramadan.

name: Qatari Khubz with Cheese and Za'atar
steps: Warm the bread on a dry pan.
Scatter with the cheese and za'atar, and drizzle with the oil.
Fold and serve warm.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread on a dry pan over medium heat, 1-2 minutes per side.
Scatter generously with the cheese and za'atar, and drizzle with the oil.
Fold in half and press briefly until the cheese softens - a common quick snack sold from Doha's corner bakeries.
description: A simple street-food snack, za'atar being a defining Levantine and Gulf spice blend.

name: Qatari Regag Crepe with Egg and Tomato
steps: Whisk the flour with enough water to make a very thin, pourable batter.
Spread thin on a hot, oiled pan, crack the egg over it, and scatter with the tomato.
Cook until set, fold, and serve.
steps_detailed: Whisk the flour with enough water to make a very thin, almost lacy batter.
Spread thin across a hot, well-oiled pan, crack the egg directly over it, season with black pepper, and scatter with the tomato.
Cook 2-3 minutes until the edges crisp and lift and the egg sets, then fold into quarters - regag sold from street carts is one of Qatar's most familiar quick bites.
description: Regag, an extremely thin, crepe-like bread, a Gulf breakfast staple.

name: Qatari Hummus with Cumin

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and cumin with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with warm flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, cumin, and garlic with a splash of water until very smooth.
Spoon into a shallow dish and drizzle with the oil - a simple, everyday mezze served at nearly every Qatari gathering.
description: A simple hummus, cumin giving it a Gulf-leaning character.

name: Qatari Legaimat

ingredients.note: tbsp, standing in for date syrup

steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, and enough water to form a thick, smooth batter. Rest 20 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until puffed and golden.
Drizzle with the honey while still warm.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, saffron, and enough warm water to form a thick, smooth batter. Cover and rest 25-30 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry, turning occasionally, 3-5 minutes until puffed and deeply golden.
Drain briefly and drizzle generously with the date syrup while still warm - legaimat is the everyday Qatari transliteration of the same beloved fried dough beloved across the Gulf.
description: Legaimat (also spelled luqaimat), small fried dough balls drizzled with date syrup, are a beloved Ramadan sweet across the Gulf.

name: Qatari Saffron Milk Pudding
steps: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk until smooth.
Warm the rest of the milk with the sugar and saffron, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer, stirring, until thickened.
Chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cornstarch into a little of the cold milk until completely smooth.

Warm the rest of the milk with the sugar, saffron, and cardamom, whisk in the cornstarch slurry, and simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 5-6 minutes until thickened to a soft, custard-like set.
Chill at least 2 hours and top with the pistachios before serving - a delicate, saffron-scented pudding common at the end of a Qatari meal.
description: A simple saffron-scented milk pudding, saffron and cardamom being common Gulf dessert flavorings.

name: Qatari Date and Tahini Sweet

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Mash the dates with the tahini until it forms a thick, sticky paste.
Roll into small balls and roll in the chopped pistachios.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Mash the dates with the tahini and cardamom until it forms a thick, sticky, well-combined paste.
Roll into small balls and roll each in the chopped pistachios to coat - a simple no-cook sweet that keeps well and travels easily to gatherings.
description: Dates and tahini, a classic Gulf sweet pairing.

name: Qatari Lime and Garlic Sauce
steps: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, and cilantro.
Serve alongside grilled or baked fish.
steps_detailed: Combine the garlic, lime juice, oil, cilantro, and cumin.
description: Let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - a bright, simple sauce that pairs especially well with baked fish.

name: Qatari Tahini Lime Drizzle

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Whisk the tahini, lime juice, garlic, and a splash of water until smooth and pourable.
Drizzle over grilled meats or vegetables.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Whisk the tahini, lime juice, garlic, cumin, and a splash of water until smooth and pourable, thinning with more water as needed.
Drizzle over grilled meats, vegetables, or warm bread.
description: A simple tahini-lime sauce.
